# Thanks for considering contributing to the TVRA project!

We welcome all forms of contributions, including but not limited to:
- 🐛 Bug reports
- 💡 Feature requests
- 📝 Documentation improvements
- 🔧 Submitting Pull Requests

---

## 📜 Code of Conduct

Please remain friendly, inclusive, and professional. All participants are expected to adhere to our [Code of Conduct](./CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md).

---

## 🎯 Development Workflow

### 1. Fork this repository
Click the "Fork" button at the top right of the GitHub page to create your own fork.

### 2. Clone your Fork
```bash
git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/Traffic-Violation-Reporting-Automation-TVRA-.git
cd Traffic-Violation-Reporting-Automation-TVRA-
```

### 3. Create a feature branch
```bash
git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name
# or
git checkout -b fix/your-bug-fix
```

### 4. Development
Develop according to the project's technology stack:
- **Python 3.12**
- **YOLO (v26)** - Image Recognition
- **OpenCV, PaddleOCR** - Image Processing
- **Selenium/Playwright** - Automation

### 5. Commit your changes
```bash
git add .
git commit -m "feat: add description of the new feature"
```

### 6. Push to your Fork
```bash
git push origin feature/your-feature-name
```

### 7. Submit a Pull Request
Submit a PR on GitHub and fill out all fields in the PR template.

---

## 📐 Coding Guidelines

### Python Code Style
- Follow the [PEP 8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/) style guide
- Use meaningful variable and function names
- Add docstrings for complex logic

### Commit Message Format
```
<type>: <subject>

<body>
```

**Types:**
- `feat`: New feature
- `fix`: Bug fix
- `docs`: Documentation changes
- `style`: Formatting changes (does not affect code execution)
- `refactor`: Refactoring
- `perf`: Performance improvements
- `test`: Testing related
- `chore`: Build/tooling changes

### Branch Naming Conventions
- `feature/` - New features
- `fix/` - Bug fixes
- `hotfix/` - Emergency fixes
- `refactor/` - Refactoring
- `docs/` - Documentation updates

---

## 🔍 Review Criteria

Your PR will be reviewed based on the following criteria:
1. ✅ Code quality and readability
2. ✅ Passes existing tests
3. ✅ Adds necessary tests
4. ✅ Complete documentation
5. ✅ Complies with the project's code style

---
